items.title: Fed and ECB Seen Diverging in French Snapshot of War Aftermath
items.summary: The Federal Reserve will still need to hike interest rates this year despite Thursday’s numbers pointing to labor-market weakness, according to two French chief economists.

items.title: Ukrainian suspect hunted by police after Monaco bomb attack was 'disguised as a man'
items.summary: Officials believe the suspect may not have acted alone and spent days casing out scene of the attack.

items.title: Four Reasons Crimea Has Become Crucial in the Ukraine War
items.summary: Escalating drone strikes, fuel shortages and power cuts in the region Russia annexed are among the factors turning up the heat on President Vladimir V. Putin.

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: Iran Prepares to Bury Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Killed at Iran War’s Outset
items.summary: Long-delayed funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, killed during U.S.-Israeli strikes at the war’s outset, are set to begin Friday. For the regime, it is a critical moment to demonstrate that it has endured.

items.title: OPEC Output Surged in June as Hormuz Flows Jumped, Survey Shows
items.summary: OPEC’s crude oil production surged last month as Persian Gulf members restored exports through the Strait of Hormuz amid a peace accord between the US and Iran, according to a Bloomberg survey.

items.title: Woman From Ukraine Was Behind Monaco Bomb Attack, Officials Say
items.summary: Anastasiia Berezovska, a 39-year-old Ukrainian, tried to kill three people with a parcel bomb on Monday, the authorities said. She remained on the run.

items.title: Carlson’s split with Republicans to deepen rifts in US right, Chinese think tank says
items.summary:
High-profile conservative commentator Tucker Carlson’s split with President Donald Trump and the Republican Party will jeopardise the party’s prospects in the US midterm elections and intensify the quarrel over its identity and direction, according to an official Chinese think tank.
The former Fox News host has repeatedly expressed his dissatisfaction with the current administration, in particular
